title: Add New Todo
user_story: As a user, I want to add new tasks to my list, so that I can keep track of what needs to be done.
acceptance_criteria: The web page displays an input field and a submit button for entering task details.
Entering text and submitting adds the task to the visible list immediately.
Submitting an empty input shows a validation message or is ignored.
Tasks are stored temporarily in memory during the current session.

title: View Todo List
user_story: As a user, I want to see all my current tasks on one page, so that I can review what I need to accomplish.
acceptance_criteria: The web page displays a clear list of all added todos.
Each todo shows its text content clearly.
If no todos exist, the page shows a friendly message like 'No tasks yet.'
Newly added todos appear in the list immediately after creation.

title: Mark Todo as Complete or Edit Text
user_story: As a user, I want to mark tasks as done or edit their text, so that my list stays accurate and up-to-date.
acceptance_criteria: Each todo in the list has an option to toggle its completion status such as a checkbox.
Completed todos are visually distinguished with strikethrough or dimmed styling.
Users can edit the text of a todo, and changes reflect immediately upon saving.
All updates persist only for the current browser session in memory.

title: Delete Todo from List
user_story: As a user, I want to delete tasks I no longer need, so that my list stays clean and focused.
acceptance_criteria: Each todo has a visible delete button or link.
Clicking delete removes the item from the list immediately without requiring a full page reload.
Deleted todos cannot be recovered within the same session.
The remaining todos shift to fill the gap smoothly.
